title: Keteleeria longibracteata
taxonomy: Keteleeria longibracteata is classified within the conifer family Pinaceae. It belongs to the genus Keteleeria and is native to China. This plant is further classified as a species within the family Pinaceae, in the genus Keteleeria, and in the subfamily Laricoideae. The classification of Keteleeria longibracteata is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Pinophyta, class Pinopsida, order Pinales, family Pinaceae, genus Keteleeria, and species Keteleeria longibracteata. The classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and characteristics within the plant kingdom.
uses: Keteleeria longibracteata is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the mountainous regions of China. Its bark, leaves, and branches are utilized for various medicinal purposes. In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is also used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Keteleeria longibracteata in modern medicine.

intro: Keteleeria longibracteata is a species of coniferous evergreen tree native to China, belonging to the family Pinaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Keteleeria longibracteata include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
